source: Personalization of Web-search Using Short-term Browsing Context

concepts.finding: Personalizes search results using short-term browsing context (recent queries, clicked results, dwell time). Short-term context via: (1) context-aware query expansion, (2) re-ranking results based on browsing history, (3) user intent inferred from recent behavior. 10-20% improvement in search relevance metrics.
concepts.implication: Keyword intent can be disambiguated using contextual signals: previous keywords in session, click behavior, time of day, device type. Same keyword ("jordan") has different intent depending on context (athlete vs country vs shoes).
concepts.open_question: Should keyword-intent mapping be context-dependent (varying by session/user signals) or static? What contextual signals are available without user-level tracking?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Context-aware keyword intent disambiguation (using session context signals) improves intent accuracy by 15%+ over static keyword-to-intent mapping for ambiguous keywords.
